nodes.en: Formulas and tables are mathematical expressions and data compilations enabling calculation and reference lookup. They compress knowledge into compact, reusable forms for engineering, science, and commerce.
nodes.cs: Vzorce a tabulky jsou matematické výrazy a datové kompilace umožňující výpočet a vyhledávání v referenčních materiálech. Komprimují vědomost do kompaktních, opakovaně použitelných forem pro inženýrství, vědu a obchod.
nodes.brief:
Formulas and tables are compact computational references: mathematical and physical expressions paired with the numeric compilations — physical constants, conversion factors, statistical distributions, material properties — needed to evaluate them without re-deriving each value from first principles. They serve engineering, science, and commerce wherever a calculation must be looked up rather than reasoned out anew.

This node holds the raw computational substrate that other reference formats consume rather than define. [[09.03]] Technical Manuals embed formula tables inside step-by-step procedures for operating or repairing a system, but the manual's purpose is the procedure, not the underlying mathematics; [[09.05]] Standards & Specifications codify the tolerances and test methods a table's values must satisfy, while this node supplies the values themselves. The constants and relations collected here originate in [[01]] Natural Sciences and are applied downstream in [[03]] Applied Sciences & Technology, where engineering calculation depends on trusted, unit-consistent source data.

A table's value rests entirely on the traceability and precision of its source. The [NIST Reference on Constants, Units, and Uncertainty](https://physics.nist.gov/cuu/Constants/) is the internationally recognized authority for fundamental physical constants, and [Wolfram MathWorld](https://mathworld.wolfram.com/) is a widely used canonical reference for mathematical formulas and identities.
